title: salix arctica var. subcordata
intro: Salix arctica var. subcordata, also known as Arctic willow, is a shrubby plant native to the Arctic tundra, characterized by its small, lanceolate leaves and red or yellowish catkins. Traditionally, the plant's bark, leaves, and roots have been used in herbal medicine, exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The medicinal properties of Salix arctica var. subcordata are said to offer relief from pain, reduce fever, and soothe digestive issues, making it a potential remedy for various health conditions. The plant is often prepared in teas, infusions, or salves, and its extracts are used in traditional medicine to promote overall well-being and alleviate various health issues.
